# Patterns & Conventions

> Patterns that work get recorded here. When agents scaffold new files or make decisions, they check this file first to stay consistent with what's already been established.

---

## CopilotForge Conventions

### File Naming

- **Agents:** lowercase, descriptive — `planner.md`, `reviewer.md`, `api-designer.md`
- **Skills:** folder per skill, always contains `SKILL.md` — `.github/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md`
- **Cookbook recipes:** `{topic}-example.{ext}` — `session-example.ts`, `auth-example.py`
- **Memory files:** plain English names — `decisions.md`, `patterns.md`
- **No frameworks in file names** — `session-example.ts` not `copilotforge-session-handler.ts`

### Skill Format

Every `SKILL.md` follows this structure:

```markdown
---
name: "skill-name"
description: "What this skill does"
---

## Trigger
When to activate this skill.

## Instructions
Step-by-step what to do.

## Reference
Supporting material or links.
```

### Agent Definition Format

Every agent `.md` file includes:

```markdown
# Agent Name — Role

## Identity
Name, role, expertise, style.

## Responsibilities
What this agent owns.

## How I Work
Patterns and principles.

## Boundaries
What I handle vs. what I don't.
```

### Cookbook Recipe Rules

- Every recipe starts with a block comment: what it does, when to use it
- All imports present — no "install this first" surprises
- Error handling included — never bare `try/catch` with no action
- Language-specific idioms — TypeScript recipes use TypeScript patterns, Python recipes use Python patterns

### Memory Updates

- Agents append to `decisions.md` after making meaningful choices
- Patterns get added to `patterns.md` when a convention is established
- Entries are timestamped and explain *why*, not just *what*
- Most recent entries go at the top (reverse chronological)
